name: Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop
name_original: Peñas de Santa María, Peña Blanca y Ermita de Santa Isabel — circular desde Olba
description: This difficult 22.5-mile mountain biking loop explores the Fuentes de Rubielos area and Camino del Cid, with 3615 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Olba are situated in the Mijares River Valley, characterized by a mountainous landscape and extensive forests. The region offers diverse terrain, from gentle country roads to challenging routes with significant elevation changes. This area provides a natural setting for mountain biking, with trails often running alongside the Mijares River. Best mountain bike trails around Olba The most popular mountain bike trail is Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This difficult route features steep climbs and exhilarating descents. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Olba Village loop from Olba, a moderate 18.5 miles (29.7 km) path. This route winds through the Mijares River Valley, offering varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuente de la Salud – Olba Village loop from La Verdeja, a 20.5 miles (33.1 km) trail leading through forests and river views, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around Olba is defined by the Mijares River Valley, surrounding mountains, and dense forests. The network offers options for all ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult technical routes. The routes in Olba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Olba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Olba?
A: Olba offers a diverse range of mountain biking trails, from gentle country roads suitable for leisurely rides to challenging routes with steep climbs, exhilarating descents, and technical sections. The trails often run alongside the clear waters of the Mijares River or wind through mountainous landscapes and lush forests.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Olba?
A: While there are no trails specifically categorized as 'easy' in our current selection, Olba does offer moderate paths that could be suitable for riders with some experience or families looking for a less extreme adventure. These routes often follow the Mijares River Valley, providing varied terrain without overly technical challenges. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olba Village loop from Olba&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in Olba?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Olba features several difficult routes with significant elevation changes and technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996623087/fuentes-de-rubielos-and-camino-del-cid-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding trail known for its steep climbs and descents. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31438356" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olba Village – The Gorge loop from Olba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Olba?
A: Mountain biking in Olba offers views of the Mijares River Valley, surrounding mountains, and dense forests. You can also encounter notable natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5646525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrechos de Chillapájaros&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4785172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijares River Narrows&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3484565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de Piedrahíta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Olba?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Olba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olba Village loop from Olba&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33876314" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Salud – Olba Village loop from La Verdeja&lt;/a&gt;, both offering varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Olba?
A: The mountain bike trails around Olba are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the Mijares River Valley, and the variety of options available for different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Olba?
A: Olba's climate generally allows for mountain biking through much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be warm, especially at midday, while winters are milder but can still have cooler days. It's always advisable to check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any facilities or picnic areas along the mountain bike routes?
A: While specific facilities vary by route, you might find amenities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2614427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albentosa Picnic Area&lt;/a&gt; in the wider region, which can be a convenient stop for a break. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides.

Q: Can I find trails that offer panoramic views in Olba?
A: Absolutely. Given Olba's mountainous geography, many trails feature sections with excellent panoramic views of the surrounding landscapes, including the Mijares River Valley and the Sierra Espuña. Routes with significant elevation gain, such as the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996623087/fuentes-de-rubielos-and-camino-del-cid-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop&lt;/a&gt;, often reward riders with stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any specific trails that follow the Mijares River?
A: Yes, the Mijares River is a prominent natural feature in Olba, and several trails run alongside its clear waters. These routes offer a gentler yet equally appealing riding experience, allowing you to enjoy the riverside scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20136811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olba Village loop from Olba&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, winds through the Mijares River Valley.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Olba area?
A: There are around 15 mountain bike trails documented in the Olba area, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. This provides a good selection for various skill levels and preferences.
tips: We are in the small town of Olba, in the Gúdar-Javalambre region of the province of Teruel and it is certainly worth a visit. We can walk or ride a bike along the side of the river next to the forests that accompany it or visit the town center, the hermitage of San Pedro, the hermitage of San Roque or the Carlos IV bridge.
This is a stunning location with dramatic panoramic views over the Area. The Sierra Javalambre are clearly seen with the ski station and observatory visible on a clear day.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop is rated as difficult. While some sections of the broader Camino del Cid are low to medium difficulty, this specific loop includes significant elevation changes and varied terrain that will challenge experienced riders. You may encounter sections requiring extra effort or even brief dismounting.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied. You'll ride on farm tracks, reconditioned railway tracks (Vías Verdes), and mountain paths. Expect sections of forest tracks, sandy dirt roads, and some rocky areas. There's a notable strong ascent through a rocky area and a fast downhill section from Fuentes de Rubielos that requires careful attention.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you would typically look for parking in or around Fuentes de Rubielos, as the loop starts and ends there. Given the rural nature, street parking in the village or designated small lots might be available.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in this region, especially on mountain biking routes. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, near livestock, or on shared paths to ensure safety for everyone and wildlife.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to ride this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Gúdar-Javalambre region can experience varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures or even snow at higher elevations. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to ride the Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop. It's a public route, though you should always respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers picturesque mountain landscapes within the Gúdar-Javalambre region. You'll pass through diverse areas including solitary landscapes with holm oaks and pine groves, orchards, and river valleys. Notable highlights include the charming Olba Village and the historic Hermitage of Santa Isabel.

faqs.question: Does this loop intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You will ride along parts of the Camino del Cid (La Conquista de Valencia E05), La Conquista de Valencia, [CIMA TE07] Fuente de Rubielos, Camino de los Losares, Sendero Morron El Estrecho, and Senda Peñarroya, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate navigation tools. Wearing a helmet is essential, and protective gear is highly recommended due to the varied and potentially challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenging sections or insider tips for this route?
faqs.answer: Be prepared for a strong ascent through a rocky area near Fuentes de Rubielos. Also, there's a fast downhill section from Fuentes de Rubielos where bikes can pick up significant speed, so approach it with care. The route is generally well-signposted, but having a GPS device with the route loaded is always advisable.

faqs.question: Is there water or food available along the Fuentes de Rubielos and Camino del Cid Loop?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through Olba Village and starts near Fuentes de Rubielos, which may offer limited amenities, it's best to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire duration of the ride. Services can be sparse in the more remote sections.
